message:
feat(core,ondisk): add ext4 indirect block addressing and btrfs subvolume objectid support

ffs-core: Implement read_ext4_indirect() for reading file data via
indirect/double-indirect/triple-indirect block pointers, enabling
support for ext4 filesystems without the EXTENTS feature. Add
resolve_indirect_block() helper for block pointer traversal. Add
subvol_objectid field to BtrfsContext (defaults to FS_TREE = 5) and
use it in walk_btrfs_fs_tree() for subvolume-aware tree resolution.

ffs-ondisk: Remove EXTENTS from Ext4IncompatFeatures::REQUIRED_V1
since indirect block addressing is now supported. Update validation
error message from "need FILETYPE+EXTENTS" to "need FILETYPE".

Update beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-aem9","title":"Fix ffs-core request-scope call-site drift","description":"Current workspace check shows ffs-core call sites/tests still passing obsolete RequestScope arguments to read_dir/read_inode_attr and omitting RequestScope for resolve_extent. Align the call sites with the current VFS/OpenFs signatures and restore cargo check.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:16:35.069932008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:18:20.424758346Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T03:18:20.424129254Z","close_reason":"Superseded: current workspace passes rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets; stale local error log only","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-amp0","title":"Add btrfs FUSE E2E tests for xattr, symlink, hard link, statfs, setattr","description":"Btrfs FUSE E2E has only 5 tests. Missing: xattr ops, symlinks, hard links, statfs, setattr/chmod, large file writes, fsync. Mirror ext4 test structure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T06:21:16.191291287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T18:56:59.617154163Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T18:56:59.617125539Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in current tree; validated with rch test listing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-b5on","title":"Implement ext4 indirect block addressing and ZERO_RANGE fixes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T22:48:02.268634162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:48:16.655433191Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:48:16.654585948Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4 indirect block addressing:
(1) resolve_indirect_block(): resolves logical blocks via 12 direct + 1 indirect + 1 double-indirect + 1 triple-indirect pointers in i_block[0..14]
(2) read_ext4_indirect(): reads file data block-by-block using indirect resolver
(3) Wired into FsOps::read() ext4 path: checks EXT4_EXTENTS_FL; if not set, uses indirect addressing
(4) Removed EXTENTS from REQUIRED_V1 incompat set — both extents and indirect now supported
Also fixed 3 bugs in review: compressed extent size overflow, decompressed size validation, inline data xattr parsing.
473 ffs-core tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","indirect","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-b80","title":"Repair: Implement scrub pipeline (detect corruption signals)","description":"Goal: detect corrupted blocks/metadata reliably.

Deliverables:
- Define signals:
  - ext4 metadata_csum / btrfs csum verification failures
  - structural invariant violations (bounds, tree ordering)
- Implement ScrubReport with per-block findings and severity.

Acceptance:
- Harness can inject corruption (flip bit) and scrub reports it deterministically.
- Scrub does not panic on corrupted data; it returns structured findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:28.702412790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:16.268015147Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:16.267938143Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b80","depends_on_id":"bd-25k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:00.916224379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b80","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:00.995317684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-bejk","title":"Fix dx_root test layout, read-directory EISDIR check, and close stale clippy bead","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:41.502809158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.140304578Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.139616074Z","close_reason":"Fixed 3 issues:
(1) ffs-core read() missing EISDIR check: reading a directory inode via FsOps::read() returned Ok with dir data instead of Err(EISDIR). Added is_dir() guard in ext4 read path. 471 ffs-core tests pass.
(2) ffs-ondisk parse_dx_root_basic test: dx_entry layout was wrong — test wrote explicit hash(4)+block(4) for entry 0, but dx_countlimit overlaps entry 0 (hash is implicit 0, only block at offset+4). Fixed test data layout.
(3) ffs-ondisk proptest dx_entries: same layout bug — restructured to match actual format with entry 0 block separate from subsequent hash+block pairs. 360 ffs-ondisk tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","correctness","ondisk"]}
 {"id":"bd-c35","title":"Track: Harness + Conformance + Parity Accounting (ffs-harness)","description":"Build the proof system: fixture generation, comparisons against Linux behavior where applicable, parity reporting, and regression gates.\n\nAcceptance: every implemented behavior has (1) a fixture or property test, (2) a harness test that exercises it end-to-end, (3) FEATURE_PARITY + harness parity numbers updated in the same change. Benchmarks are added only after fixtures exist.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:52.148014837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385993623Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385971391Z","close_reason":"All 5 sub-tasks closed: ext4 fixtures (bd-2zg), parity accounting (bd-6st), fixture workflow (bd-3q4), kernel reference capture (bd-2ij), btrfs fixtures (bd-2yi).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2ij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.179975971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2yi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.099617897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.014650837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-3q4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.923718680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-6st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.825671893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-c35","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ffs-harness is the proof engine.

Rules:
- every new behavior needs fixtures or properties
- parity numbers must match FEATURE_PARITY.md
- only optimize after correctness vectors exist

Without this track, progress becomes vibes instead of verified parity.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
